rationale: This page contains significant legal and governance information, specifically the highlights of the Immigration and Foreigners Act, 2025, and details regarding the Land Registration System in India following a Supreme Court ruling. These are high-value topics for competitive examinations.
cleanedText:
o Specific religious minorities (Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis, and Christians) from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Pakistan who entered by December 31, 2024 (even with invalid documents), and
o Registered Sri Lankan Tamil nationals who took shelter by January 9, 2015.
• Visa exemption also applied to:
o Foreigners holding diplomatic/official passports (where waived by agreement)
o Those eligible for visa-on-arrival
o Certain foreign military personnel visiting on naval warship
Key Highlights of Immigration and Foreigners Act, 2025
• Consolidation of Laws: Replaces four statutes- Passport (Entry into India) Act, 1920; Registration of Foreigners Act, 1939; Foreigners Act, 1946; and Immigration (Carriers’ Liability) Act, 2000.
• Immigration Posts: Central Government empowered to notify designated posts for entry and exit.
• Bureau of Immigration: Established to handle visa issuance and regulate entry, transit, stay, and movement of foreigners.
• Registration Requirement: Mandatory registration of foreigners with a Registration Officer upon arrival.
• Reporting Obligations: Carriers, educational institutions, and medical institutions must furnish information relating to foreigners.
• Offences & Penalties: Entry without valid travel documents punishable with up to 5 years’ imprisonment, ₹5 lakh fine, or both.
• Power of Arrest: Police officers of Head Constable rank or above may arrest without warrant.
• Control of Premises: Civil authorities can restrict foreigners’ access to, or order closure of, places frequented by them.
8.5. LAND REGISTRATION SYSTEM IN INDIA
Why in the News?
Recently, Supreme Court in Samiullah v. State of Bihar case has called for a fundamental reform in country's land registration and titling system.
More on the News
• Supreme Court of India struck down Bihar's rule making mutation proof mandatory for property registration, reaffirming that registration records transaction, not ownership.
o It reiterated that document registration under the Registration Act, 1908, only records a transaction, while mutation (updating revenue records) is a separate process that doesn't confer title.
o Supreme Court in K Gopi v Sub Registrar Case 2025 held Registration Act, 1908 does not empower sub-registrars to check title or ownership with their only mandate being to verify that the paperwork is in order and the parties have signed it voluntarily.
Land Registration System in India
• Land is a “State subject” and registration of deeds is a concurrent list subject under the Schedule VII of the Constitution.
o India follows Deeds land registration system.
